insight: Non-prompt defense (like Doomed Wall) significantly weakens burst strategies by forcing attackers to commit resources earlier or accept worse timing.
author: cogito3
context: Discussion of defensive unit choices against aggressive strategies.

insight: Fibroid Hulk is valuable defense primarily because it threatens 2 damage, forcing opponents to respect the possibility of absorption and creating gambit opportunities, even when it ultimately soaks rather than attacks.
author: cogito3
context: Analysis of Fibroid Hulk's defensive utility and psychological impact in soak scenarios.

insight: Lancetooth is worth building primarily when denying absorb, since its lost swords would otherwise be absorbed. Without absorb denial, it functions as a strictly worse Tarsier variant.
author: consensus
context: Evaluation of when Lancetooth's unique build time and sword generation mechanics justify its cost.

insight: Lancetooth's 2-turn buildtime allows chaining two on consecutive turns when lacking extra attack, unlike units like Bloodrager that immediately generate attack and create awkward purchasing patterns.
author: willflame
context: Comparison of Lancetooth's buildtime mechanics versus other red attackers.

insight: Thunderhead is cost-efficient for soak value (~3 infusion grids worth) but requires obtaining multiple turns of attack and absorption to justify its high cost; absorb denial makes it significantly worse.
author: cogito3, Wilson, Greyman
context: Resource cost analysis of Thunderhead comparing it to alternative defense options.

insight: Doomed Drones should almost always be built instead of regular drones when you are certain they will gain full defensive value, as the gold efficiency is superior and cascades into more Doomed Drones.
author: medar, supergreyman, shadourow
context: Discussion of optimal drone choice when opponent has sufficient attack to justify defensive value.

insight: In third-engineer games, P2 should play DE (Doomed Drone then Engineer) on turn 1 because Doomed Drones gain full defensive value against P1's turn 1 attack while being extremely gold-efficient.
author: shadourow, medar
context: Optimal P2 opening strategies when third Engineer is viable.

insight: Shredder should only be purchased after sufficient opponent damage exists (roughly 4+ damage plus your absorb potential) to prevent it from being abused for extra defense that doesn't block; it should almost never be clicked for defense.
author: medar, supergreyman
context: Guidelines on when to build Shredder and proper usage to avoid inefficiency.

insight: Walls should generally not be bought unless needed immediately, as preemptive wall purchases are inefficient; prioritize sinks like Shredder or Splitter instead when dumping blue.
author: medar
context: Efficiency discussion of defensive unit purchasing patterns.

insight: DD/DA (fastimus) is a strong P2 opening in sets without green, trading a drone for early attackers; it gets ~11 resources to spend on DTT or similar purchases and generally gives P2 the upper hand.
author: star_bringer, medar
context: Discussion of non-standard P2 openings when third Engineer is not viable.

insight: Attacking with a single unit to deal just 1 damage early is usually inefficient because the opponent will absorb it anyway; holding the unit for a turn to make a perfectly-sized attack that denies absorb is typically better.
author: willflame, medar
context: Analysis of attack timing and the importance of making absorb-denying damage increments.

insight: Quad-red units like Tatsu Nullifier, Lucent Hellion, and Apollo make sets feel faster than they appear; units like Shadowfang and Vai Mauronax can come from double Flame Animus but are not as inherently fast.
author: mrguy888, darkeisbein
context: Analysis of which advanced red units accelerate game pace.

insight: Ebb Turbine should never be clicked to buy a Drone in normal play because it wastes a unit slot and blue resource with no net effect; only theoretical edge cases like Golden Armor make this play relevant.
author: amalloy
context: Clarification that Ebb Turbine clicking to generate Drones is not a valid strategy.

insight: Spending 2 attack to kill a unit grants the opponent approximately 4 gold equivalent value, making unit-killing trades a form of economic resource exchange that must be evaluated carefully.
author: cachotognax
context: Discussion of the resource cost of spending attack on killing opponent units.

insight: Iceblade Palisman is the strongest unit in sets with it; the winning strategy is typically to set up BBA (Blastforge + 2x Blue sinks) and achieve two Iceblades per turn for overwhelming defense.
author: cogito3
context: Meta assessment of Iceblade's dominance in certain unit pools.

insight: Wild Drone allows P1 to achieve 1C (one Drone into Conduit) opening, enabling earlier access to key attackers like Tantalum Ray or Trinity without sacrificing economy.
author: supergreyman, willflame
context: Opening flexibility provided by Wild Drone in early game resource management.

insight: When Conduit is available, a second Conduit can be more valuable than building Animus in certain sets, as it increases gold generation for purchasing key attackers like Tantalum Ray or Forcefield.
author: willflame
context: Economic choice between additional Conduit versus Animus expansion.

insight: Feral Warden can serve as red sink that also defends, making it superior to Steelsplitter in sets where you want to attack more than defend, avoiding overcommitment to Blastforge.
author: supergreyman
context: Alternative blue sink and defense options to Steelsplitter in certain sets.

insight: Botch Pillar should almost always be bought instead of Tarsier when available, as it provides superior value; in a set with good Botch availability, first priority should be multiple Botches before considering Tarsiers.
author: medar
context: Unit choice priority between common attackers in mixed sets.

insight: Iso Kronus is a better attacker than Tarsier in certain circumstances, particularly when its synergies (like with Bite) create cascading damage; however, Iso requires additional tech investment compared to Tarsier.
author: cogito3, cachotognax
context: Comparison of Iso Kronus versus Tarsier as primary attackers depending on synergy.

insight: When opponent has Hannibull and will rush it, Tarsier's build time can deny absorb on that unit; however, if you can transition to better attackers, the timing advantage may not justify staying on Tarsiers.
author: cogito3, cachotognax
context: Specific counter-strategy against Hannibull rushing.

insight: In high-economy sets, cutting a drone early (DD DB instead of DD DDE) to accelerate Flame Animus should only be done if the set is not too high-econ; in very high-econ sets, a third Engineer plus later Flame Animus is often superior.
author: amalloy, cogito3
context: Opening decision when Flame Animus exists in economically strong unit pools.

insight: Supply (unit availability) functions as a resource in Prismata; currently, nothing benefits from low supply, and having higher unit supply always dominates having lower supply.
author: namington, cogito3
context: Discussion of whether unit supply depletion can have value under Smooth Dominant Resilience principle.

insight: A defense position with an extra unit of supply (e.g., 10 Engineers vs 9 Engineers) dominates equivalent defenses with fewer in supply, assuming all else is equal.
author: namington
context: Supply efficiency principle in defensive positioning.
